caveats: inputs.ai_replaceable_share: Supplied role mix is 3-digit 523 and includes brokerage/asset-management contamination.
inputs.ai_replaceable_share: AI use is not equivalent to headcount removal.
inputs.pi_dist: Fee schedules and client mix are target-specific.
inputs.pi_moat: Client-consent rules create retention risk, not automatic moat.
inputs.t50_years: Measured use is above 50%, but effective adoption is a stricter construct.
inputs.current_adoption_pct: Not a direct census measure; prevents incorrectly setting t50 to zero.
inputs.historical_analogs: Not a causal estimate of future fee compression.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct: No direct owner-age statistic; do not treat adviser demographics as owner demographics.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct.succession_shortage_documented: This is market commentary, not a code-specific succession survey.
inputs.active_consolidators: Count is an estimate of active platforms/strategics, not reported by either source.
inputs.buy_mult: AGS's transaction median is not a size-matched small-firm entry comp; quality small sellers may trade nearer the market median.
inputs.buy_mult: Owner compensation and contingent retention economics must be normalized before using this estimate in a live deal.
inputs.exit_mult: The observed median spans RIA transactions and is not a size- or quality-matched forecast for a particular consolidated exit.

inputs.exit_mult: The report states larger and higher-quality firms receive higher valuations; a founder-dependent small target may require a discount after owner-compensation and client-retention normalization.
inputs.pricing_structure: No exact fee-rate selection is used in scoring.
dataset_inputs.n_band: Form ADV adviser census is not NAICS 523940 and excludes state-registered advisers.
dataset_inputs.n_band: The 0.8% fee and distribution within AUM bands are judgmental bridges; no published AUM-band table was reopened.
dataset_inputs.n_band: The supplied 11.65% margin is broad asset-management, likely below standardized wealth-RIA margins; n_band is intentionally low confidence.

S1 and S6 support AI adoption and affected workflows but do not directly establish the AUM-fee prevalence or retention mechanism used in pi_dist and pricing_structure; those selections remain estimates.

S4's 11.0x median adjusted-EBITDA multiple comes from AGS's 37-transaction 2024 sample and is not a size- or quality-matched exit forecast; M is therefore highly sensitive to target normalization and transaction mix.

q.rationale: The existing v3 distribution-retention × moat-retention judgment is reused as a bounded Stage 1 commercial-retention proxy.

leaves.rationale: Predeclared bounded demand/operator row for the inherited v3 terminal class; unknown classes remain missing.

e.rationale: Global eligible-union share; heterogeneous records use the predeclared wider/lower class-count row.

s5.rationale: Fixed transform of the disclosed v3 owner-age range into five-year qualifying control-transfer propensity.

a.rationale: AI targets documented administrative and client-communication workflows while client relationships, fiduciary judgment and rainmaking remain.

l.detail: QCEW 2024 private total annual wages at 5239 ($166,457,424,125) x benefits multiplier 1.4294 [ECEC private-industry total comp $46.60/hr / wages $32.60/hr = 1.4294 (March 2026 reference period)] / SUSB 2022 receipts at 5239 ($454,212,096,000). Vintage mismatch: 2024 wages over 2022 receipts, stated. Level: ancestor 5239. Harmonized to IPS basis ÷1.3407 (median of 65 dual-method codes).

rho.rationale: Fixed outcome-blind transform of the v3 t50 adoption estimate, widened by the declared bridge error.

source_review.summary: Mechanics, exact identity/model routing, manifest digests, dataset injection, arithmetic, fresh finalization and fresh memo rendering all pass. All six registered sources are accessible. The direct adoption, adviser-population, deal-volume, buyer-count, assignment-consent and 11.0x adjusted-EBITDA claims are supported; remaining weaknesses are disclosed estimates or non-fatal source/scope limitations. No reversed factor semantics or capture/terminal double count is likely to change the thesis: client-consent risk is treated as a transferability constraint, and the PRESSURED terminal cap does not bind the current conditional score. The useful conclusion remains publishable only with all 18 authored caveats plus the two validator caveats, especially because M, n_band, owner age and capture can move the near-cut result.

sources.title: 2025 RIA Benchmarking Study | Charles Schwab
sources.what_it_supports: Schwab's 1,288-firm 2025 study reports 68% of firms use AI in some way; the data are self-reported.

sources.title: Investment Adviser Association Industry Statistics
sources.what_it_supports: 2026 IAA/COMPLY Form ADV snapshot reports 16,544 advisers in 2025, 92.8% with 100 or fewer employees, 67.4% below $1B AUM, and individual-client firms averaging 8 employees and $424M AUM.

sources.title: ECHELON 2025 RIA M&A Deal Report
sources.what_it_supports: ECHELON reports 2025 wealth-management deal volume above 400 and a 17.6% year-over-year increase in unique buyers; it also describes elevated smaller transaction demand.

sources.title: The RIA Deal Room 2025 | Advisor Growth Strategies
sources.what_it_supports: AGS reports that its 2024 RIA transaction data had a median adjusted EBITDA multiple of 11.0x, up 11% from 2023; it also explains that valuation rises with scale and seller quality, and that the largest platforms can command 20x adjusted EBITDA or more.

sources.title: SEC Investment Management Staff Issues of Interest — Advisory Contracts
sources.what_it_supports: SEC describes Advisers Act section 205(a)(2) contract provisions requiring client consent to assignment for registered advisers.

sources.title: 2026 RIA & AI Research Study: Advisor AI in Action | Charles Schwab
sources.what_it_supports: Schwab's October 2025 survey of 533 advisors reports 63% use AI, most are experimenting, and impact is most felt in administrative tasks and client communications.

title: Portfolio Management and Investment Advice
